title: Ovarian Health. Multi-organ Nutrigenomic Effects of Dietary Grapes in a Mouse Model. In a year-long study where mice consumed a grape-enriched diet, eating the equivalent of 2.5 servings of grapes per day, study findings showed the ability of grapes to modulate gene expression in the ovaries. Consuming grapes enriched an array of beneficial pathways and down-regulated harmful pathways, including the up-regulation of genes related to the process of ovulation and down-regulation of genes involved in ovarian cancer.

sentences.text: Multi-organ Nutrigenomic Effects of Dietary Grapes in a Mouse Model.

In a year-long study where mice consumed a grape-enriched diet, eating the equivalent of 2.5 servings of grapes per day, study findings showed the ability of grapes to modulate gene expression in the ovaries.

Consuming grapes enriched an array of beneficial pathways and down-regulated harmful pathways, including the up-regulation of genes related to the process of ovulation and down-regulation of genes involved in ovarian cancer.
